name: Jay Leonhart
description: American double bassist, singer, and songwriter
abstract: Jay Leonhart (born December 6, 1940) is an American double bassist, singer, and songwriter who has worked in jazz and popular music. He has performed with Judy Garland, Bucky Pizzarelli, Carly Simon, Frank Sinatra, and Sting.

interviews.title: Jay Leonhart interviewed by Monk Rowe, San Diego, California, February 15, 1998 [interview 1 of 2]

about_quotes.speaker: Alan Raph
about_quotes.evidence: And the first band... had Faddis and Soloff playing trumpets and Jimmy and I were playing trombone... and Jay Leonhart played bass. It was a really good band that he called the New York All-Star Band.
about_quotes.doc_title: Alan Raph interviewed by Monk Rowe, Danbury, Connecticut, November 20, 2001

about_quotes.speaker: Al Gafa
about_quotes.evidence: I work with a trio... Jay Leonhart is the bass player... I did it with Jay and a piano player by the name of Ted Rosenthal.
about_quotes.doc_title: Al Gafa interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, May 2, 2023

about_quotes.speaker: Nicki Parrott
about_quotes.evidence: Jay Leonhart subs for me. ... And it was a star cast of bass players. I was petrified. It was Jay [Leonhart], Michael Moore, I think Phil Flanigan, Milt wasn’t there, this was a set for Milt.
about_quotes.doc_title: Nicki Parrott interviewed by Monk Rowe. Utica, New York, November 6, 2010 [interview 1 of 2]

about_quotes.speaker: Winard Harper
about_quotes.evidence: Jay Leonhart and I have been on some gigs together. The last time I worked with him we might have done Dizzy’s with George Wein.
about_quotes.doc_title: Winard Harper interviewed by Monk Rowe, Utica, New York, October 10, 2014

about_quotes.speaker: Bucky Pizzarelli
about_quotes.evidence: LRC Records with New York Swing, it’s John Bunch and Jay Leonhart, just a trio
about_quotes.doc_title: Bucky Pizzarelli interviewed by Monk Rowe, Aspen, Colorado, October 11, 1997 [interview 1 of 2]

about_quotes.speaker: John Pizzarelli
about_quotes.evidence: when he worked with John Bunch and Jay Leonhart, they would let — Bucky would talk for a while and then he’d say, “John Bunch” and John would say, “Here’s what we’re going to do next.”
about_quotes.doc_title: John Pizzarelli interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, October 18, 2023 [interview 2 of 2]
